micro_segments.title: Rava's Challenge: If Tumtum is Safek, It Should Be Included in Erech

macro_segments.title: Shelamim Sacrifice Exclusion: Androgynos Cannot Be Brought as an Offering

micro_segments.title: Neveilah Status: Androgynos and Tumtum Birds Render Unfit and Impure on Consumption

macro_segments.title: Temurah Disqualification: Androgynos Cannot Receive or Convey Kedusha

micro_segments.title: Five Categorically Excluded Animals: Treifah, Kilayim, Yotzei Dofen, Androgynos, Tumtum

macro_segments.title: Tannaitic Debate on Androgynos: Male with Mum vs. Third Gender vs. Textual Exclusion

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yishmael: Androgynos is a Male with a Blemish, Workable Labor Forbidden
Chachamim Disagree: No Kedusha Falls on Androgynos; It Is a Third Gender
Rabbi Shimon Ben Yehuda: Textual Derivation Excludes Both Tumtum and Androgynos

macro_segments.title: Tumtum Investigation: Urine Location as Evidence for Male or Female Determination

micro_segments.title: Zachar Nekeivah Hu D'Mesafkah: Both Remain Doubtful Until Investigated
Urine from Male Location Proves Zachar; Urine from Female Location Disputed

macro_segments.title: Case of Tumtum Urinating from Female Location: Is It Definite Nekeivah or Safek?

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Ila's Ruling: Animal Urinating Posteriorly Is Definite Nekeivah, Not Bechor
Rabbi Yochanan's Objection: How Ignore Mishna's Consensus That Tumtum Is Safek?

Pesak Halacha: Resh Lakish Determines Gender by Urine Location in Animals, Not Humans
Rabbi Oshaya's Challenge: Cannot Ignore Possibility Organ Is Bent Despite Urination Clue

micro_segments.title: Ishtani D'Ishtani: When Body Is Abnormal, All Assumptions About It Are Questionable

macro_segments.title: Tumtum in Marriage Law: Kiddushin, Chalitzah, and Yibum Obligations

micro_segments.title: Tumtum Marrying Woman: Binding Kiddushin as Safek; Chalitzah and Yibum Both Required
Woman Marrying Tumtum: Wife Must Have Chalitzah But Cannot Do Yibum
Concern for Saris Status: Is Tumtum Assumed to Be Sterile Due to Covered Genitalia?
Rabbi Yehuda: Tumtum That Is Found to Be Male Is Definitely Sterile (Saris)
Rabbi Yossi ben Rabbi Yehuda: Tumtum Might Be Found Female or Possibly Sterile
Practical Difference: If Sterile Definitely vs. Possibly, Other Brothers' Rights to Yibum

macro_segments.title: Summary: Range of Positions on Androgynos and Tumtum Classification

micro_segments.title: Three Androgynos Categories: Intrinsically Indeterminate Safek vs. Male with Mum vs. Third Gender
